Title: System Migration from On-Prem AD to Azure AD  for JDH
Notes: JDH-HQ-PC-02 Pre-Migration: Reviewed the system and documented necessary information for restoration. Captured screenshots for reference. Backed up user profile data and Desktop files to OneDrive. Synced OneDrive to ensure all files were up to date. Exported browser bookmarks with stored passwords to a separate location. .Confirmed all required data and information were secured. Unjoin system from on-premises AD and rebooted the machine. Migration: Logged in with local administrator account after reboot. Joined the machine to Azure AD using Global Administrator credentials. Updated user's Azure AD password and verified successful login. Post-Migration: Restored all backups (profile, Desktop files).Restored browser bookmarks along with stored passwords. Configured applications: Outlook, Teams, OneDrive. Result: System successfully migrated to Azure AD. All user data restored and functioning properly.

Title: Failed patches updated on machine  JDH-HQ-PC-09
Notes: As per the alert connected to JDH-HQ-PC-09 download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.and installed failed patches as well. Google Chrome (x64) (146.0.7680.80), Adobe Acrobat Reader DC (x64) (25.001.21288), Microsoft Edge for chromium business (146.0.3856.62) (x64) (CVE-2026-3909), Update for Microsoft WebView2 Runtime (x64) (146.0.3856.62), Update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Business Current Channel for x64 2602 of version(19725.20172)

Title: Re: JDH Pacific, Inc. PC-DMIS Order Confirmation PO # 1303
Notes:
Received an email from Henry S. regarding the Hexagon software update license and his availability to connect for troubleshooting. I initially replied asking for permission to connect remotely, but did not receive a response.

Following Rishad's request, I proceeded to connect to Henry's computer to resolve the issue. Upon accessing the system, I discovered that the drive had limited available space. The following steps were taken:

  1. TreeSize Analysis:
    I downloaded and ran TreeSize to analyze the storage on the system. The tool helped identify large files and unnecessary data consuming space.

  2. Data Cleanup:
    Unnecessary files, including old software patches and temporary data, were deleted. This helped free up space, increasing the available storage from 2GB to approximately 11.1GB.

  3. System Update:
    After completing the disk cleanup, I updated Rishad on the progress and confirmed that the necessary steps had been taken to resolve the storage issue.

Current Status:
The storage issue on Henry's system has been resolved. The available space has been increased to around 11.1GB. The system is now functioning with more free space. I have updated Rishad for further process and resolution.

Title: Failed Veeam backups for JDH
Notes: While monitoring the backups for JDH, cloud backups got failed. SO connected to the server and troubleshoot the issue. The issue with the expired certificate for the cloud backup job was resolved by updating the SSL certificate on the Veeam Backup and Replication server. The new certificate was imported into the appropriate certificate store on the server, ensuring it was correctly associated with the myveeamspc.technijian.com service provider. After restarting the necessary Veeam services, the repository was rescanned, and the backup job for the cloud repository successfully ran without any further certificate errors.

Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - JDH-HQ-VONE: (10.0.0.155) - Disk Utilization is 70%, threshold value for this monitor is 60%
Notes: As per the alert connected to JDH-HQ-VONE, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 46% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

Title: RE: Computer issue
Notes:
Received an email from Rishad regarding a low disk space issue on the system. As per the request, connected remotely to the machine to investigate and resolve the issue.

  1. Initial Assessment:
    • Checked the disk usage and found that the C: drive was running low on space, which could impact system performance.
  2. Cleanup Activities Performed:
    • Deleted unnecessary temporary files (Temp and %Temp%) to free up space.
    • Used TreeSize to analyze disk usage and identified large/unnecessary files and folders.
    • Removed unwanted data to reclaim storage space.
  3. System Optimization:
    • Ran SFC (System File Checker) scan to ensure system file integrity.
    • Reviewed and adjusted Page File (Virtual Memory) settings to optimize performance and reduce load on the C: drive.
  4. System Restart:
    • Restarted the system to apply all changes and ensure proper functionality.
  5. Final Outcome:
    • Successfully freed up disk space, and the available space on the system is now approximately 8 GB.
    • System performance improved after cleanup and optimization.
  6. Communication:
    • Provided an update to Rishad regarding the completed work and current system status.

Current Status:
Disk space issue has been resolved, system is stable, and sufficient space has been created. The ticket is now closed.
